plugin.name: Epsilon_Inventory_Monitor
plugin.author: Epsilon
plugin.purpose: Live inventory display window for Aardwolf

trigger.name: emon_ingame
trigger.match: ^Welcome to Aardwolf\. May your adventures be mystical

muclient.script:
require "aardwolf_colors" -- StylesToColours / strip_colours / ColoursToANSI (xterm-aware) require "themed_miniwindows" -- ThemedTextWindow require "gmcphelper" -- gmcp()
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- constants -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- local TYPE_CONTAINER = 11
-- one CSV line of invdata/invitem output (name field may contain commas/colour -- codes; the 5 trailing numeric fields anchor the match) local invrex = rex.new("^(\\d+),(\\w*),(.+),(\\d+),(\\d+),([01]),(-?\\d+),(-?\\d+)")
local TYPE_NAME = { [1]="Light",[2]="Scroll",[3]="Wand",[4]="Stave",[5]="Weapon",[6]="Treasure", [7]="Armor",[8]="Potion",[9]="Furniture",[10]="Trash",[11]="Container", [12]="Drink",[13]="Key",[14]="Food",[15]="Boat",[16]="Mob Corpse", [17]="Player Corpse",[18]="Fountain",[19]="Pill",[20]="Portal",[21]="Beacon", [22]="Gift Card",[23]="Unused",[24]="Raw Material",[25]="Campfire", [26]="Forge",[27]="Runestone", }
-- flags letter -> display info (from the 'object auras' help). N (Nolocate) is -- letter -> display info, from the in-game object-flags help (the @ colour on -- each entry there IS the inventory colour). N (Nolocate) is in the invdata flags -- field but the inventory omits it, so it's skipped. Blue Aura's short code is -- cyan in the help (to set it apart from Magic's blue), hence its short_colour. local FLAG_INFO = { K = { short = "(K)", long = "(K)", colour = "@R" }, M = { short = "(M)", long = "(Magic)", colour = "@B" }, G = { short = "(G)", long = "(Glow)", colour = "@W" }, H = { short = "(H)", long = "(Hum)", colour = "@C" }, I = { short = "(I)", long = "(Invis)", colour = "@w" }, C = { short = "(C)", long = "(Cursed)", colour = "@D" }, E = { short = "(E)", long = "(Envenomed)", colour = "@G" }, T = { short = "(T)", long = "(Tempered)", colour = "@R" }, B = { short = "(B)", long = "(Blue Aura)", colour = "@B", short_colour = "@c" }, R = { short = "(R)", long = "(Red Aura)", colour = "@R" }, } local FLAG_SKIP = { N = true } local SEPARATOR = "@x242" .. string.rep("-", 120)
-- fixed-location ("scope") windows beyond the main inventory, toggled by -- `emon keyring` / `emon vault`. Same line format as invdata; different command -- and tags. `var` is the persisted visibility flag. local SCOPES = { keyring = { loc = "keyring", title = "Keyring", cmd = "keyring data", var = "keyring_visible", x = 80, y = 120, always_open = true }, vault = { loc = "vault", title = "Vault", cmd = "vault data", var = "vault_visible", x = 80, y = 500 }, } local SCOPE_ORDER = { "keyring", "vault" }
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- state -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- local items = {} -- id -> record (with .loc and .seq) local seq_counter = 0 -- assigns a stable display order (approx inventory order) local main_window -- ThemedTextWindow or nil local monitors = {} -- key = lowercased container term -> monitor table
local reconcile_pending = false local suppress_close_hook = false -- true while WE delete a window (vs. user clicking [X]) local entered = false -- our own reliable "in-game" flag (GMCP can be stale) local golive_pending = false local cap = nil -- the active capture batch ({loc,lines,on_done,...}) or nil local batch_queue = {} -- batches waiting for the current one to finish local batch_gen = 0 -- generation counter for the batch timeout safety local scope_win = {} -- loc ("keyring"/"vault") -> ThemedTextWindow
-- persisted display settings local show_flags = (GetVariable("show_flags") == "1") -- default off local use_short_flags = (GetVariable("short_flags") or "1") == "1" -- default short local sort_mode = GetVariable("sort_mode") or "inv" -- "inv" | "type" -- expiry-timer colour thresholds, in hours: < red -> red, > yellow -> green, -- anything in between -> yellow. Defaults 24h (1 day) / 168h (7 days). local timer_red_h = tonumber(GetVariable("timer_red_h")) or 24 local timer_yellow_h = tonumber(GetVariable("timer_yellow_h")) or 168 -- for a stack of timed items, show the "soonest" (next to drop) or "latest" -- (when you run out) member's expiry. Tick = expiry-countdown redraw period (s). local stack_timer_mode = GetVariable("stack_timer") or "soonest" -- "soonest" | "latest" local timer_tick_secs = tonumber(GetVariable("timer_tick_secs")) or 1
-- forward declarations local make_record, parse_invdata, store_item, remove_item, relocate, collect local is_monitored_cid, aggregate, sort_stacks, matches_any local format_count, format_flags, format_level, format_row, render_stacks local make_window, quiet_delete, ensure_main_window, find_container local render_main, render_window_loc, render_monitor, render_monitor_missing, render_all local ensure_scope, toggle_scope local rebuild_container, emon_batch local schedule_render, schedule_reconcile, toggle_main, note, show_help local schedule_timer_tick, schedule_live_render
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- helpers -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- function note(msg) ColourNote("cyan", "", "[emon] ", "silver", "", tostring(msg)) end
-- Our own "in-game" flag. We do NOT read GMCP for this: MUSHclient keeps stale -- char.base/char.status across a disconnect, so right after a reconnect GMCP still -- says "playing" while you're sitting at the username prompt. `entered` is set only -- by the in-game welcome line (or a genuine reload while playing) and cleared on -- connect/disconnect. Gates every MUD command so nothing leaks to the login screen. local function in_game() return entered end
-- GMCP "actively playing" states (per aard_inventory_serials). Trustworthy only -- when GMCP is current (a plugin reload while playing), NOT right after a reconnect. local function gmcp_playing() local s = tonumber(gmcp("char.status.state")) return s == 3 or s == 8 or s == 9 or s == 11 end
-- captured invdata lines arrive styled (invdata "breaks down" colours); recover -- the @-coded text, robust against a literal '@' in a name local function styleline_to_text(line) for _, run in ipairs(line) do run.text = run.text:gsub("@", "\0") end local text = StylesToColours(line):sub(3) return (text:gsub("%z", "@")) end
function make_record(w) return { id = w[1], flags = w[2], name = w[3], -- keeps colour codes (for display) plain = strip_colours(w[3]), -- codes stripped (for matching) level = tonumber(w[4]), itype = tonumber(w[5]), unique = (w[6] == "1"), wearloc = tonumber(w[7]), timer = tonumber(w[8]), } end
function parse_invdata(style_lines) local records = {} for _, line in ipairs(style_lines) do local _, _, w = invrex:match(styleline_to_text(line)) if w then records[#records + 1] = make_record(w) end end return records end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- item state -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- function store_item(rec, loc) local existing = items[rec.id] rec.loc = loc if existing then rec.seq = existing.seq -- keep display order across updates else seq_counter = seq_counter + 1 rec.seq = seq_counter end -- anchor the decay timer to wall-clock now (timer is seconds-remaining at pull -- time; -1 = no timer). expiry is absolute, so the display can count down -- client-side between pulls without re-issuing commands. Re-anchors on every -- pull (a re-store) since that's the only fresh source of the seconds value. if rec.timer and rec.timer >= 0 then rec.expiry = os.time() + rec.timer else rec.expiry = nil end items[rec.id] = rec end
function remove_item(id) items[id] = nil end
function is_monitored_cid(cid) for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do if mon.cache_id == cid then return true end end return false end
-- move a known item; if unknown and it's entering a scope we display, resync function relocate(id, loc) local it = items[id] if it then it.loc = loc elseif loc == "inv" or is_monitored_cid(loc) then schedule_reconcile() end end
-- records matching a predicate, in stable display order function collect(pred) local out = {} for _, it in pairs(items) do if pred(it) then out[#out + 1] = it end end table.sort(out, function(a, b) return a.seq < b.seq end) return out end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- aggregate / filter (display pipeline) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- function aggregate(records) local bykey, order = {}, {} for _, r in ipairs(records) do local key = r.name .. "\1" .. tostring(r.level) local st = bykey[key] if not st then st = { name = r.name, plain = r.plain, level = r.level, itype = r.itype, flags = r.flags, count = 0 } bykey[key] = st order[#order + 1] = st end st.count = st.count + 1 -- a stack shows a SINGLE timer collapsed from its members: "soonest" (when -- the count next drops) or "latest" (when you run out). Stacked identical -- items each keep their own record (keyed by objectid) with its own timer; -- only the displayed tag collapses to one. if r.expiry then if not st.expiry then st.expiry = r.expiry elseif stack_timer_mode == "latest" then if r.expiry > st.expiry then st.expiry = r.expiry end else if r.expiry < st.expiry then st.expiry = r.expiry end end end end return order end
function sort_stacks(stacks) -- v1: original (seq) order; swap here for other sorts return stacks end
function matches_any(plain, terms) local lower = plain:lower() for _, t in ipairs(terms) do if lower:find(t, 1, true) then return true end end return false end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- row formatting (matches the MUD's inventory layout) -- <count col, 7 wide><flags?>@w<name> @W(@G<level>@W) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- count field, padded to a fixed pixel width so names line up. With a monospace -- font this reproduces the old 7-char field exactly (every glyph is one space -- wide). With a proportional font there are no tab stops in a flow renderer, so -- it pads to the nearest whole space -- columns are approximate, jitter <= one -- space. Metrics are cached per window and re-measured when its font changes. function format_count(win, count) local token = (count > 1) and ("(" .. count .. ")") or "" local fk = win.textrect.font_name .. "/" .. win.textrect.font_size if win._emon_fk ~= fk then win._emon_fk = fk win._emon_ctw = win:text_width("(9999) ") -- target field width win._emon_spw = math.max(1, win:text_width(" ")) -- one space, never zero end local tokw = (token ~= "") and win:text_width(token) or 0 local pad = math.max(1, math.ceil((win._emon_ctw - tokw) / win._emon_spw)) return "@W" .. token .. string.rep(" ", pad) end
function format_flags(flags) local parts = {} for i = 1, #flags do local c = flags:sub(i, i) if not FLAG_SKIP[c] then local info = FLAG_INFO[c] if info then local col = (use_short_flags and info.short_colour) or info.colour parts[#parts + 1] = col .. (use_short_flags and info.short or info.long) end end end if #parts == 0 then return "" end return table.concat(parts, use_short_flags and "" or " ") .. " " end
function format_level(level) return "@W(@G" .. tostring(level) .. "@W)@w" end
-- duration shown down to seconds from the highest non-zero unit (e.g. -- "13d6h42m18s", "6h0m18s", "42m18s", "18s"). Always carrying seconds is -- deliberate: a 14-day key would otherwise sit on "14d" for an hour before the -- display changed -- showing the seconds lets you SEE it counting down. local function format_duration(sec) local d = math.floor(sec / 86400) local h = math.floor((sec % 86400) / 3600) local m = math.floor((sec % 3600) / 60) local s = sec % 60 if d > 0 then return d .. "d" .. h .. "h" .. m .. "m" .. s .. "s" end if h > 0 then return h .. "h" .. m .. "m" .. s .. "s" end if m > 0 then return m .. "m" .. s .. "s" end return s .. "s" end
-- colour by remaining seconds: < red threshold -> red, > yellow -> green, else -- yellow. Boundaries chosen so every value lands in exactly one band (no gaps). local function timer_colour(sec) if sec < timer_red_h * 3600 then return "@R" end if sec > timer_yellow_h * 3600 then return "@G" end return "@Y" end
-- "[1d6h]" coloured by urgency; "[expired]" (red) if already past local function format_timer_tag(expiry) local sec = expiry - os.time() local label = (sec > 0) and format_duration(sec) or "expired" return timer_colour(sec) .. "[" .. label .. "]@w" end
function format_row(win, st) local row = format_count(win, st.count) if show_flags then row = row .. format_flags(st.flags) end -- reset to @w before the name so a name with no leading colour code doesn't -- inherit the last flag's colour row = row .. "@w" .. st.name .. " " .. format_level(st.level) if st.expiry then row = row .. " " .. format_timer_tag(st.expiry) end return row end
function render_stacks(win, stacks) if sort_mode ~= "type" then for _, st in ipairs(stacks) do win:add_text(format_row(win, st), false) end return end local groups, types = {}, {} for _, st in ipairs(stacks) do local g = groups[st.itype] if not g then g = {}; groups[st.itype] = g; types[#types + 1] = st.itype end g[#g + 1] = st end table.sort(types, function(a, b) return (TYPE_NAME[a] or ("Type " .. a)) < (TYPE_NAME[b] or ("Type " .. b)) end) for _, tnum in ipairs(types) do win:add_text(SEPARATOR, false) win:add_text("@x214Item Type: @W" .. (TYPE_NAME[tnum] or ("Type " .. tnum)), false) for _, st in ipairs(groups[tnum]) do win:add_text(format_row(win, st), false) end end if #types > 0 then win:add_text(SEPARATOR, false) end end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- windows -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- per-window font: defaults to the MUD's monospace output font until the user -- picks one via the window's right-click "Change Font" item. Stored per target -- ("main", "keyring", "vault", "mon_<key>") so each window is independent -- e.g. -- an 8pt overview alongside a 14pt single-potion monitor. local function get_font(target) local n = GetVariable("font_" .. target .. "_name") local s = tonumber(GetVariable("font_" .. target .. "_size")) if not n or n == "" then n = GetAlphaOption("output_font_name") end -- MUD output font if not s then s = GetOption("output_font_height") end return n, s end
local function set_font_var(target, name, size) SetVariable("font_" .. target .. "_name", name) SetVariable("font_" .. target .. "_size", tostring(size)) end
-- target: persistence key for get_font/set_font_var. render_fn: redraw this -- window's contents (called after a font change, with the new metrics). function make_window(id, left, top, w, h, title, target, render_fn, on_close) local font_name, font_size = get_font(target) local win = ThemedTextWindow( id, left, top, w, h, title, "center", -- title alignment true, -- is_temporary -> [X] close button true, -- resizeable true, -- text_scrollable true, -- text_selectable true, -- text_copyable false, -- url_hyperlinks false, -- autowrap (one item per line) nil, nil, font_name, -- per-target body font (default: MUD monospace output font) font_size, 1000, nil, false, nil, nil, "Close" ) -- built-in "Change Font" item, like the stat monitor's right-click menu. The -- native font picker, then loadFont changes the body font in place (no window -- recreate, so position/menu/hotspots survive), persist it, and re-render. table.insert(win.menu_table, 1, "Change Font") table.insert(win.menu_handlers, 1, function() local cn, cs = get_font(target) local picked = utils.fontpicker(cn, cs) if picked and picked.name then set_font_var(target, picked.name, picked.size) win.textrect:loadFont(picked.name, picked.size) if render_fn then render_fn() end end end) if on_close then local base = win.do_on_delete win.do_on_delete = function(self) if base then base(self) end if not suppress_close_hook then on_close() end end end return win end
function quiet_delete(win) if not win then return end suppress_close_hook = true win:delete() suppress_close_hook = false end
function ensure_main_window() if not main_window then main_window = make_window(GetPluginID() .. "_main", 400, 120, 320, 420, "Inventory", "main", render_main, function() main_window = nil SetVariable("main_visible", "0") end) end end
-- first carried container (type 11, loc "inv") whose name contains `term` function find_container(term) local needle = term:lower() local invs = collect(function(it) return it.loc == "inv" and it.itype == TYPE_CONTAINER end) for _, it in ipairs(invs) do if it.plain:lower():find(needle, 1, true) then return it.id, it.plain, it.name end end return nil end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- rendering (reads from `items`; no commands) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- rebuild a window's body while preserving its scroll position. clear+re-add -- follows the bottom (TextRect lockstep), so without this any redraw jumps a -- scrolled window to the bottom -- and on the timer tick that showed as a flicker -- (draw-at-bottom, then restore). One draw at the kept position; the scrollbar -- re-syncs from start_line via the draw's update callbacks. local function repaint(win, build) local scroll = win.textrect:getScroll() win:clear(false) build() win.textrect:setScroll(scroll, true) -- set start_line, no extra draw win:draw() end
-- render the items at a given loc into a window (used by main / keyring / vault) function render_window_loc(win, loc) if not win then return end repaint(win, function() local stacks = sort_stacks(aggregate(collect(function(it) return it.loc == loc end))) if #stacks == 0 then win:add_text("@D(empty)", false) else render_stacks(win, stacks) end end) end
function render_main() render_window_loc(main_window, "inv") end
function render_monitor_missing(mon) local win = mon.window if not win then return end win:clear(false) win:add_text("@RNo carried container matching '@w" .. mon.term .. "@R'.@w", false) win:draw() end
function render_monitor(mon) local win = mon.window if not win then return end local cid, _, cname = find_container(mon.term) mon.cache_id = cid if not cid then render_monitor_missing(mon); return end
local recs = collect(function(it) return it.loc == cid end) if #mon.filters > 0 then local kept = {} for _, r in ipairs(recs) do if matches_any(r.plain, mon.filters) then kept[#kept + 1] = r end end recs = kept end local stacks = sort_stacks(aggregate(recs)) local total = 0 for _, st in ipairs(stacks) do total = total + st.count end
repaint(win, function() local hdr = (cname or mon.term) if #mon.filters > 0 then hdr = hdr .. " @D[@w" .. table.concat(mon.filters, ", ") .. "@D]" end hdr = hdr .. " @W(" .. total .. ")@w" win:add_text(hdr, false) if #stacks == 0 then win:add_text("@D(none)", false) else render_stacks(win, stacks) end end) end
function render_all() render_main() for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do render_window_loc(scope_win[SCOPES[name].loc], SCOPES[name].loc) end for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do render_monitor(mon) end end
-- scope windows (keyring / vault): fixed-location displays toggled by command function ensure_scope(sc) if not scope_win[sc.loc] then scope_win[sc.loc] = make_window(GetPluginID() .. "_" .. sc.loc, sc.x, sc.y, 280, 360, sc.title, sc.loc, function() render_window_loc(scope_win[sc.loc], sc.loc) end, function() scope_win[sc.loc] = nil; SetVariable(sc.var, "0") end) end end
function toggle_scope(sc) if scope_win[sc.loc] then quiet_delete(scope_win[sc.loc]); scope_win[sc.loc] = nil SetVariable(sc.var, "0") elseif not in_game() then note("Open the " .. sc.title .. " window once you're in-game.") else -- pull first; open the window only if data actually came back, so `emon -- vault` with no vault just shows the MUD's message and opens nothing emon_batch({ sc.cmd }, function() local got = false for _, it in pairs(items) do if it.loc == sc.loc then got = true; break end end -- keyring (always_open) opens even when empty -- a keyring is something -- you always have, the MUD says nothing when it's empty, and an empty -- window reads better than a silent no-op. vault opens only on data: "no -- vault" is a real distinct state the MUD announces itself, so a duplicate -- [emon] line would just be noise. if got or sc.always_open then ensure_scope(sc) SetVariable(sc.var, "1") render_window_loc(scope_win[sc.loc], sc.loc) end end) end end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- batch capture: send `config compact YES` + `config prompt NO` (GMCP), fire a -- set of data commands, then `echo {emon-done}` -- all at once, so the config is -- in effect before any command runs. Each {tag}...{/tag} block in the reply is -- captured, omitted, and dispatched to rebuild_container(loc, records); anything -- else (a channel line, etc.) passes through. The sentinel restores config and -- ends the batch. Config is toggled ONCE per batch (no per-command toggling, so -- no GMCP-read race) and there is no blank-eater. See the emon_cap_* triggers. -- (Uses plain `echo`; a level 202+ immortal would need `echo self`.) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- local function cap_teardown() EnableTrigger("emon_cap_start", false) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_end", false) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_body", false) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_sentinel", false) end
-- Config is suppressed ONCE and held across all chained batches, then restored -- only when the queue finally drains. (Reading config per batch caught a stale -- GMCP value mid-restore: round 2 saw the not-yet-arrived restore, thought prompts -- were already off, skipped re-suppressing, and its prompts leaked. The v0.5 bug.) local cfg_orig = nil -- {compact, prompt} captured when suppression began; nil = not suppressed
local function suppress_config() if cfg_orig then return end -- already suppressed for this run of batches cfg_orig = { compact = gmcp("config.compact") or "NO", prompt = gmcp("config.prompt") or "YES" } if cfg_orig.compact ~= "YES" then Send_GMCP_Packet("config compact YES") end if cfg_orig.prompt ~= "NO" then Send_GMCP_Packet("config prompt NO") end end
local function restore_config() if not cfg_orig then return end if cfg_orig.compact ~= "YES" then Send_GMCP_Packet("config compact " .. cfg_orig.compact) end if cfg_orig.prompt ~= "NO" then Send_GMCP_Packet("config prompt " .. cfg_orig.prompt) end cfg_orig = nil end
local function start_batch(b) batch_gen = batch_gen + 1 cap = { loc = nil, lines = {}, on_done = b.on_done, gen = batch_gen } suppress_config() -- sets config once; a no-op for chained batches (round 2, etc.) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_start", true) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_end", true) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_sentinel", true) for _, c in ipairs(b.commands) do SendNoEcho(c) end SendNoEcho("echo {emon-done}") DoAfterSpecial(8, "Emon_batch_timeout(" .. batch_gen .. ")", sendto.script) -- safety net end
local function finish_batch() local done = cap and cap.on_done cap_teardown() cap = nil if done then done() end -- may queue the next batch if batch_queue[1] then start_batch(table.remove(batch_queue, 1)) -- more batches: keep config suppressed else restore_config() -- all done: restore config end end
-- queue a batch (one runs at a time). `commands` is a list of command strings. function emon_batch(commands, on_done) if not in_game() then return end batch_queue[#batch_queue + 1] = { commands = commands, on_done = on_done } if not cap then start_batch(table.remove(batch_queue, 1)) end end
-- capture trigger handlers (global: named by the emon_cap_* triggers' script=) function Emon_cap_start(name, line, wildcards) if not cap then return end local tag, id = wildcards[1], Trim(wildcards[2] or "") if tag == "invdata" then cap.loc = (id ~= "" and id) or "inv" elseif tag == "eqdata" then cap.loc = "worn" elseif tag == "keyring" then cap.loc = "keyring" elseif tag == "vault" then cap.loc = "vault" end cap.lines = {} EnableTrigger("emon_cap_body", true) StopEvaluatingTriggers(true) -- don't let the catch-all body re-process this tag end
function Emon_cap_body(name, line, wildcards, styles) if cap then cap.lines[#cap.lines + 1] = styles end end
function Emon_cap_end(name, line, wildcards) EnableTrigger("emon_cap_body", false) if cap and cap.loc then rebuild_container(cap.loc, parse_invdata(cap.lines)) cap.loc, cap.lines = nil, {} end StopEvaluatingTriggers(true) end
function Emon_cap_sentinel(name, line, wildcards) finish_batch() StopEvaluatingTriggers(true) end
function Emon_batch_timeout(g) -- global: DoAfterSpecial safety net if cap and cap.gen == tonumber(g) then finish_batch() end end
-- Rebuild a scope (loc = "inv" / "worn" / a container id / "keyring" / "vault") -- from an authoritative data pull: store the records, drop any at that loc that -- are gone, redraw. The batch capture dispatches every block through here. function rebuild_container(loc, records) local seen = {} for _, rec in ipairs(records) do store_item(rec, loc); seen[rec.id] = true end for id, it in pairs(items) do if it.loc == loc and not seen[id] then items[id] = nil end end schedule_render() end
function refresh_all() -- global: in-game baseline, `emon refresh`, reconcile if not in_game() then return end -- never pull at the login screen if not main_window and not next(monitors) and not next(scope_win) then return end -- round 1: carried inventory + worn gear + any open scope windows local r1 = { "invdata", "eqdata" } for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do if scope_win[SCOPES[name].loc] then r1[#r1 + 1] = SCOPES[name].cmd end end emon_batch(r1, function() -- round 2: the contents of every carried container (ids learned in round 1) local r2 = {} for _, it in ipairs(collect(function(i) return i.loc == "inv" and i.itype == TYPE_CONTAINER end)) do r2[#r2 + 1] = "invdata " .. it.id end if #r2 > 0 then emon_batch(r2, render_all) else render_all() end end) end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- debounced render (live) and reconcile (resync) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- schedule_render: synchronous, for the baseline / just-created-window path. -- (A deferred draw via DoAfterSpecial does not repaint reliably right after a -- window is created, which left windows blank until a manual toggle forced a -- direct render.) function schedule_render() render_all() end
-- schedule_live_render: debounced redraw for the live invmon/invitem stream. A -- burst (`put all.token bag`, emptying a keyring, etc.) fires one invmon per -- item; redrawing on each was the lag. Item state is still updated per-event -- (instantly) -- only the redraw is coalesced into one, ~0.25s after the burst -- starts (and at most once per 0.25s during a sustained stream). Windows already -- exist on this path, so the deferred-draw caveat above doesn't apply. local live_render_pending = false function schedule_live_render() if live_render_pending then return end live_render_pending = true DoAfterSpecial(0.25, "Emon_live_render()", sendto.script) end
function Emon_live_render() -- global: DoAfterSpecial target live_render_pending = false render_all() end
function schedule_reconcile() if reconcile_pending then return end reconcile_pending = true DoAfterSpecial(0.3, "Emon_do_reconcile()", sendto.script) end
function Emon_do_reconcile() -- global: DoAfterSpecial target reconcile_pending = false refresh_all() end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- expiry-timer tick: a self-rescheduling redraw (NO commands sent) so the -- countdowns visibly tick down between pulls. The seconds value only refreshes -- on an actual pull (`emon refresh`, reopening keyring, etc.); this just recomputes -- the display from each item's stored expiry. Runs while in-game; only repaints -- when something on screen actually has a timer. -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- local timer_tick_scheduled = false
-- is any item displayed at `loc` carrying a live expiry timer? local function loc_has_expiry(loc) for _, it in pairs(items) do if it.loc == loc and it.expiry then return true end end return false end
-- same, for a monitor: a timed item in its container that passes the name filter local function mon_has_expiry(mon) if not mon.cache_id then return false end for _, it in pairs(items) do if it.loc == mon.cache_id and it.expiry and (#mon.filters == 0 or matches_any(it.plain, mon.filters)) then return true end end return false end
function schedule_timer_tick() if timer_tick_scheduled then return end timer_tick_scheduled = true DoAfterSpecial(timer_tick_secs, "Emon_timer_tick()", sendto.script) end
function Emon_timer_tick() -- global: DoAfterSpecial target timer_tick_scheduled = false if not in_game() then return end -- loop stops on disconnect; go-live restarts it -- redraw ONLY windows that currently show a timed item, so a timer-less window -- is never cleared/redrawn (no needless work, no flicker). render_* preserves -- scroll, so no save/restore needed here. if main_window and loc_has_expiry("inv") then render_main() end for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do local w = scope_win[SCOPES[name].loc] if w and loc_has_expiry(SCOPES[name].loc) then render_window_loc(w, SCOPES[name].loc) end end for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do if mon.window and mon_has_expiry(mon) then render_monitor(mon) end end schedule_timer_tick() -- keep ticking; timers may appear/refresh end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- live event handlers (no commands) -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- {invmon}action,objectid,containerid,wear-loc function emon_on_invmon(name, line, wildcards) local a, oid, cid = wildcards[1]:match("^(%d+),(%d+),(-?%d+)") local action = tonumber(a) if not action then return end
if action == 3 or action == 7 then remove_item(oid) -- dropped/given/consumed elseif action == 2 then relocate(oid, "worn") -- worn elseif action == 6 then relocate(oid, cid) -- into a container elseif action == 9 then relocate(oid, "vault") -- put into vault elseif action == 11 then relocate(oid, "keyring") -- put onto keyring elseif action == 1 or action == 4 or action == 5 or action == 10 or action == 12 then relocate(oid, "inv") -- (un)worn / added / out of container / from vault/keyring else return end schedule_live_render() end
-- {invitem}id,flags,name,... (literal @-codes; full data for a genuine add) function emon_on_invitem(name, line, wildcards) local _, _, w = invrex:match(wildcards[1]) if w then store_item(make_record(w), "inv") schedule_live_render() end end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- commands -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- local function cmd_monitor(rest) rest = Trim(rest or "") -- container term: a quoted "..."/'...' string (allows spaces, so you can tell -- apart two containers that share a keyword like "bag"), else the first -- whitespace-delimited word. The remainder is the optional comma name list. local term, namelist local q = rest:sub(1, 1) if q == '"' or q == "'" then term, namelist = rest:match("^" .. q .. "([^" .. q .. "]+)" .. q .. "%s*(.*)$") else term, namelist = rest:match("^(%S+)%s*(.*)$") end if not term or term == "" then note("Usage: emon monitor <container> [name,list] (quote to match multiple words: \"Bag of Aardwolf\")") return end local key = term:lower() local filters = {} for w in (namelist or ""):gmatch("[^,]+") do local t = Trim(w):lower() if t ~= "" then filters[#filters + 1] = t end end
if monitors[key] and monitors[key].window then quiet_delete(monitors[key].window) end local offset = 0 for _ in pairs(monitors) do offset = offset + 1 end local mon_target = "mon_" .. key:gsub("%W", "") local win = make_window(GetPluginID() .. "_" .. mon_target, 740, 120 + offset * 30, 260, 320, term, mon_target, function() render_monitor(monitors[key]) end, function() monitors[key] = nil end) monitors[key] = { term = term, filters = filters, window = win }
note("Monitoring '" .. term .. "'" .. (#filters > 0 and (" for: " .. table.concat(filters, ", ")) or "") .. ".")
-- baseline just this container's contents (one pull, not a full resync) local cid = find_container(term) monitors[key].cache_id = cid if cid then emon_batch({ "invdata " .. cid }, function() render_monitor(monitors[key]) end) else render_monitor(monitors[key]) end end
local function cmd_unmonitor(rest) local key = Trim(rest or "") -- accept the same quoting as `monitor` (a multi-word term also works unquoted, -- since the whole remainder is the key) key = (key:match('^"(.+)"$') or key:match("^'(.+)'$") or key):lower() if key == "" then note("Usage: emon unmonitor <container>"); return end local mon = monitors[key] if not mon then note("No monitor named '" .. key .. "'."); return end quiet_delete(mon.window) monitors[key] = nil note("Stopped monitoring '" .. key .. "'.") end
-- a threshold token: a number of hours, or with a d/h/m/s suffix. Returns hours -- (fractional for m/s). e.g. "7d"->168, "24h"/"168"->that, "10m"->1/6, "30s"->1/120 local function parse_hours(s) local num, unit = s:match("^([%d%.]+)([dhms]?)$") num = tonumber(num) if not num then return nil end if unit == "d" then return num * 24 end if unit == "m" then return num / 60 end if unit == "s" then return num / 3600 end return num -- "h" or no suffix end
local function hours_str(h) -- compact threshold label, zero units omitted: "7d" / "1d12h" / "10m" / "30s" local secs = math.floor(h * 3600 + 0.5) local d = math.floor(secs / 86400); secs = secs % 86400 local hh = math.floor(secs / 3600); secs = secs % 3600 local mm = math.floor(secs / 60) local ss = secs % 60 local out = "" if d > 0 then out = out .. d .. "d" end if hh > 0 then out = out .. hh .. "h" end if mm > 0 then out = out .. mm .. "m" end if ss > 0 then out = out .. ss .. "s" end return (out ~= "") and out or "0s" end
local function cmd_timer(rest) rest = Trim(rest or "") if rest ~= "" then local a, b = rest:match("^(%S+)%s+(%S+)$") local red = a and parse_hours(a) local yellow = b and parse_hours(b) if not red or not yellow then note("Usage: emon timer <red> <yellow> (hours, or use d/h: 'emon timer 1d 7d')") return end if red <= 0 or yellow <= 0 or red >= yellow then note("Need 0 < red < yellow (red is the more-urgent, smaller threshold).") return end timer_red_h, timer_yellow_h = red, yellow SetVariable("timer_red_h", tostring(red)) SetVariable("timer_yellow_h", tostring(yellow)) render_all() end -- AnsiNote (not note) so the colour words actually render in their colour AnsiNote(ColoursToANSI( "@C[emon]@w Expiry colours -- @Rred@w under " .. hours_str(timer_red_h) .. ", @Yyellow@w from " .. hours_str(timer_red_h) .. " to " .. hours_str(timer_yellow_h) .. ", @Ggreen@w over " .. hours_str(timer_yellow_h) .. ".")) end
local function cmd_stacktimer(rest) local mode = Trim(rest or ""):lower() if mode == "" then stack_timer_mode = (stack_timer_mode == "latest") and "soonest" or "latest" -- toggle elseif mode == "soonest" or mode == "latest" then stack_timer_mode = mode else note("Usage: emon stacktimer [soonest|latest]") return end SetVariable("stack_timer", stack_timer_mode) note("Stacked items show the " .. stack_timer_mode .. " expiry (" .. (stack_timer_mode == "latest" and "when you run out" or "when the count next drops") .. ").") render_all() end
local function cmd_tickrate(rest) local arg = Trim(rest or "") if arg ~= "" then local n = tonumber(arg) if not n or n < 1 then note("Usage: emon tickrate <seconds> (whole seconds, 1 or more)") return end timer_tick_secs = math.floor(n) SetVariable("timer_tick_secs", tostring(timer_tick_secs)) schedule_timer_tick() -- ensure the loop is running; new period applies from the next tick end note("Expiry countdown redraws every " .. timer_tick_secs .. "s.") end
-- dump as much internal state as is useful for diagnosing behaviour. `emon debug` -- shows the whole state machine (lifecycle, GMCP, settings, capture, windows, item -- tallies); `emon debug items` adds a line per tracked item (can be 100+ lines). local function cmd_debug(rest) local function p(s) AnsiNote(ColoursToANSI(s)) end local function yn(b) return b and "@Gy@w" or "@Rn@w" end local arg = Trim(rest or ""):lower()
local total, with_timer, by_loc, list = 0, 0, {}, {} for _, it in pairs(items) do total = total + 1 by_loc[it.loc] = (by_loc[it.loc] or 0) + 1 if it.expiry then with_timer = with_timer + 1 end list[#list + 1] = it end
p("@C===== emon debug =====@w") p("@Wlifecycle@w connected=" .. yn(IsConnected()) .. " in_game=" .. yn(in_game()) .. " entered=" .. yn(entered) .. " gmcp_playing=" .. yn(gmcp_playing()) .. " golive_pending=" .. yn(golive_pending) .. " reconcile_pending=" .. yn(reconcile_pending)) p("@Wgmcp@w state=" .. tostring(gmcp("char.status.state")) .. " name=" .. tostring(gmcp("char.base.name")) .. " config.compact=" .. tostring(gmcp("config.compact")) .. " config.prompt=" .. tostring(gmcp("config.prompt"))) p("@Wdisplay@w flags=" .. yn(show_flags) .. " short_flags=" .. yn(use_short_flags) .. " sort=" .. sort_mode) p("@Wtimers@w red=" .. timer_red_h .. "h yellow=" .. timer_yellow_h .. "h stack=" .. stack_timer_mode .. " tickrate=" .. timer_tick_secs .. "s tick_scheduled=" .. yn(timer_tick_scheduled) .. " items_with_timer=" .. with_timer) if cap then p("@Wcapture@w ACTIVE loc=" .. tostring(cap.loc) .. " lines=" .. #cap.lines .. " gen=" .. tostring(cap.gen) .. " queued=" .. #batch_queue .. " batch_gen=" .. batch_gen) else p("@Wcapture@w idle queued=" .. #batch_queue .. " batch_gen=" .. batch_gen) end p("@Wmain win@w " .. (main_window and "@Gopen" or "@Rclosed") .. "@w") local sc = {} for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do sc[#sc + 1] = name .. "=" .. (scope_win[SCOPES[name].loc] and "open" or "closed") end p("@Wscope wins@w " .. table.concat(sc, " ")) local nmon = 0 for _ in pairs(monitors) do nmon = nmon + 1 end p("@Wmonitors@w " .. nmon) for key, mon in pairs(monitors) do p(" @Y" .. key .. "@w term='" .. mon.term .. "' filters=[" .. table.concat(mon.filters, ",") .. "] cid=" .. tostring(mon.cache_id) .. " win=" .. (mon.window and "open" or "closed")) end p("@Witems@w total=" .. total .. " seq_counter=" .. seq_counter) local locs = {} for loc in pairs(by_loc) do locs[#locs + 1] = loc end table.sort(locs) for _, loc in ipairs(locs) do local citem = items[loc] local label = citem and (loc .. " (" .. citem.plain .. ")") or loc p(" @Wloc " .. label .. ":@w " .. by_loc[loc]) end if arg == "items" then table.sort(list, function(a, b) return a.seq < b.seq end) for _, it in ipairs(list) do local left = it.expiry and format_duration(math.max(0, it.expiry - os.time())) or "-" p(" @D#" .. it.seq .. "@w id=" .. it.id .. " loc=" .. tostring(it.loc) .. " ty=" .. it.itype .. "(" .. (TYPE_NAME[it.itype] or "?") .. ") timer=" .. tostring(it.timer) .. " left=" .. left .. " @w" .. it.plain) end else p("@D(emon debug items -- list every tracked item)@w") end end
function show_help() local function p(coded) AnsiNote(ColoursToANSI(coded)) end local function center(s) -- centre s inside an 80-wide @c box row local inner = 78 local left = math.floor((inner - #s) / 2) return "@c|" .. string.rep(" ", left) .. s .. string.rep(" ", inner - #s - left) .. "|" end local bar = "@c" .. string.rep("-", 80)
p("") p(bar) p(center("Epsilon's Inventory Monitor")) p(center("Vibe coded with Claude, but doesn't suck.")) p(bar) p("@Yemon") p("@c Toggle all inventory windows.") p("@c") p("@Yemon monitor @W<container> @c[@Wnames@c]") p("@c Tracks a carried container in its own window.") p("@c") p("@W <container>@c matches the NAME of a container you carry, e.g. @Wbag@c or") p("@c @W'bag of aardwolf'@c. Accepts quotes for multiple words, first match wins.") p("@c Note: It does *not* accept 2.'foo bar' syntax at this time.") p("@c") p("@W [names]@c is a comma-separated NAME LIST matched against displayed") p("@c item names (NOT the keyword). For example:") p("@c @Yemon monitor 'Bag of Aardwolf' duff, Cobra Blood") p("@c This shows bag items whose name contains 'duff' or 'Cobra Blood' in the") p("@c monitored window. Quotes around the item names to monitor are") p("@c unnecessary, even when multi-word.") p("@c") p("@c Color codes and case are ignored when matching, so it should work") p("@c whether you're copying with colors or not.") p("@c") p("@Yemon unmonitor @W<container>") p("@c Closes a monitor window (you can also click the ! in the top-left") p("@c corner of a window to close it)") p("@c") p("@Yemon keyring") p("@c Toggle a window showing your keyring contents.") p("@c") p("@Yemon vault") p("@c Toggle a window showing your vault contents. Returns the stock error from") p("@c \"vault list\" if you don't have one.") p("@c") p("@Yemon sort @W[inv|type]") p("@c Choose between displaying windows in inventory order or grouped by item") p("@c type. Type order is like the \"invsort type\" command. Applies to all") p("@c windows.") p("@c") p("@Yemon flags") p("@c Toggles display of item flags on/off.") p("@c") p("@Yemon shortflags") p("@c Toggles short @W(G)@c vs long @W(Glow)@c flags, similar to the") p("@c 'shortflags' command.") p("@c") p("@Yemon timer @c[@Wred yellow@c]") p("@c Items with a timer (i.e. keys) show the amount of time left. The default") p("@c display shows items with less than 24 hours left in @Rred@c, 24 to 168") p("@c hours (1 to 7 days) in @Yyellow@c, and more than 168 hours in @Ggreen@c.") p("@c") p("@c The two thresholds can be set as desired, using d/h/m/s to specify days,") p("@c hours, minutes, or seconds. A number with no identifier assumes hours.") p("@c Example:") p("@c @Yemon timer 10m 168@c") p("@c") p("@c The above would set the lower threshold to 10 minutes, and the upper") p("@c threshold to 168 hours (7 days).") p("@c") p("@c Using @Yemon timer@c shows the current thresholds.") p("@c") p("@Yemon stacktimer @c[@Wsoonest|latest@c]") p("@c By default, items show their timer and color based on the @Wsoonest@c") p("@c item expiry. This command can change it to base the color and timer on") p("@c the @Wlatest@c item in the stack to expire instead. Without an argument,") p("@c the command toggles the display type.") p("@c") p("@Yemon tickrate @c[@Wseconds@c]") p("@c How often the item countdown display redraws in windows (default 1s). If") p("@c things feel laggy with a lot of keys, try setting this higher to reduce") p("@c how often it refreshes.") p("@c") p("@Yemon refresh") p("@c Resyncs all inventory from the game. Use this if something's acting weird.") p("@c") p("@Yemon invmon") p("@c Re-enables the \"invmon\" tags. This should happen on startup, but this is") p("@c a reminder for if it doesn't work. Also, if you uninstall, you'll need") p("@c to enter \"invmon\" to turn the tags off.") p("@c") p("@Yemon help") p("@c This helpfile. Consider yourself PKed.") p(bar) p("@w") end
function emon_command(name, line, wildcards) local args = Trim(wildcards[1] or "") local sub, rest = args:match("^(%S*)%s*(.*)$") sub = (sub or ""):lower()
if sub == "" then toggle_main() elseif sub == "monitor" then cmd_monitor(rest) elseif sub == "unmonitor" or sub == "unmon" then cmd_unmonitor(rest) elseif sub == "keyring" then toggle_scope(SCOPES.keyring) elseif sub == "vault" then toggle_scope(SCOPES.vault) elseif sub == "sort" then local mode = Trim(rest):lower() if mode == "" then sort_mode = (sort_mode == "type") and "inv" or "type" elseif mode == "inv" or mode == "type" then sort_mode = mode else note("Usage: emon sort [inv|type]"); return end SetVariable("sort_mode", sort_mode) note("Sort: " .. (sort_mode == "type" and "grouped by item type." or "inventory order.")) render_all() elseif sub == "flags" then show_flags = not show_flags SetVariable("show_flags", show_flags and "1" or "0") note("Item flags " .. (show_flags and "shown." or "hidden.")) render_all() elseif sub == "shortflags" then use_short_flags = not use_short_flags SetVariable("short_flags", use_short_flags and "1" or "0") note("Flag labels: " .. (use_short_flags and "short, e.g. (K)." or "long, e.g. (Kept).")) render_all() elseif sub == "timer" then cmd_timer(rest) elseif sub == "stacktimer" then cmd_stacktimer(rest) elseif sub == "tickrate" then cmd_tickrate(rest) elseif sub == "refresh" then refresh_all() elseif sub == "invmon" then SendNoEcho("invmon on") note("Sent 'invmon on'.") elseif sub == "debug" then cmd_debug(rest) elseif sub == "help" then show_help() else note("Unknown command '" .. sub .. "'. Try 'emon help'.") end end
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- lifecycle -- --------------------------------------------------------------------- -- -- enter the game for real: enable tags, open the window, baseline. Guarded by -- in_game() (our own flag), with a small delay so the room/login output settles. function Emon_go_live() -- global: DoAfterSpecial target golive_pending = false if not in_game() then return end SendNoEcho("invmon on") -- enable live tags; never turned off if (GetVariable("main_visible") or "1") == "1" then ensure_main_window() for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do if GetVariable(SCOPES[name].var) == "1" then ensure_scope(SCOPES[name]) end end end refresh_all() -- baseline state from the MUD schedule_timer_tick() -- start the expiry countdown redraw loop end
-- go live shortly, de-duped (welcome line + a reload check can coincide) local function schedule_golive() if golive_pending then return end golive_pending = true DoAfterSpecial(0.25, "Emon_go_live()", sendto.script) end
-- the in-game "Welcome to Aardwolf" line: our reliable "you're in now" signal function emon_on_ingame() entered = true schedule_golive() end
-- master visibility toggle for ALL emon windows (main + monitors) function toggle_main() if (GetVariable("main_visible") or "1") == "1" then if main_window then main_window:hide() end for _, w in pairs(scope_win) do w:hide() end for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do if mon.window then mon.window:hide() end end SetVariable("main_visible", "0") else ensure_main_window() main_window:show() for _, name in ipairs(SCOPE_ORDER) do if GetVariable(SCOPES[name].var) == "1" then ensure_scope(SCOPES[name]) end end for _, w in pairs(scope_win) do w:show() end for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do if mon.window then mon.window:show() end end SetVariable("main_visible", "1") if next(items) then render_all() else refresh_all() end end end
function OnPluginConnect() entered = false -- at the login screen; ignore stale GMCP, wait for the welcome line end
function OnPluginDisconnect() entered = false end
function OnPluginInstall() -- (re)loaded: if we loaded while already playing (a reload, so GMCP is current, -- not stale), go live now. A fresh login is handled by the welcome-line trigger. if IsConnected() and gmcp_playing() then entered = true schedule_golive() end end
function OnPluginEnable() OnPluginInstall() end
function OnPluginClose() quiet_delete(main_window) main_window = nil for _, w in pairs(scope_win) do quiet_delete(w) end scope_win = {} for _, mon in pairs(monitors) do quiet_delete(mon.window) end monitors = {} end
function OnPluginDisable() OnPluginClose() end
function OnPluginListChanged() dofile "lua/aard_requirements.lua" end
